data.ir-exec-summary:
On June 4th, 2026, starting at approximately 9:31 AM, an outside attacker gained access to Synaplex Neurotechnologies’ computer systems. They did this by sending one of our researchers, Dr. Morty Smith, a fake login approval request that appeared to come from a legitimate cloud service. By approving the request, the researcher unknowingly handed the attacker a valid pass into our environment.

Over the next 13 days, the attacker moved deeper into our systems without triggering any response. By June 9th, they had gained the same level of access as our own IT administrators. They deliberately disabled key security tools to avoid detection, then copied and removed an estimated 47.3 gigabytes of our most sensitive research: the NeuraRoute neuro-delivery program, which includes materials prepared under a government research contract.

The breach was discovered on June 17th, 2026, when an external security service flagged a suspicious website the attacker had been using. A review of historical records confirmed the attacker had been active for nearly two weeks before anyone was alerted.
data.ir-lessons-sustain:
The commercial threat intelligence subscription and SIEM IOC ingestion pipeline performed well once triggered; retroactive SIEM match across 11 days of proxy logs enabled rapid scoping of the initial compromise and full kill-chain reconstruction within hours of declaration.

Microsoft Defender for Identity fired accurate, high-fidelity alerts for Pass-the-Hash, Kerberoasting, DCSync, and Golden Ticket usage. These are load-bearing detection signals worth investing in; the workflow around actioning them must be improved, not the detections themselves.
data.ir-lessons-improve:
1. Alert triage and response SLA enforcement: A Falcon HIGH alert for process hollowing sat in queue for 11 days. Implement mandatory escalation timers: HIGH alerts unactioned after 4 hours auto-escalate to L2 lead; unactioned after 8 hours page the on-call IR lead. Defender for Identity high-severity alerts require a documented, tested response runbook.

2. Device code phishing as a Conditional Access gap: Block the OAuth 2.0 device authorization grant flow for all non-privileged users via Conditional Access policy. Enforce admin consent workflow for applications requesting Mail.ReadWrite or Files.ReadWrite.All permissions. These two controls together would have blocked the initial access vector and the NeuraSyncPro OAuth persistence independently.

3. Service account least privilege; eliminate DA-member Kerberoastable SPNs: Any account with a registered SPN that holds Domain Admins, Enterprise Admins, or Schema Admins membership must be identified and privilege-reduced immediately. svc-portal-wubba@synaplexbio.com was a one-step full domain compromise waiting to happen. Implement a quarterly SPN-to-privilege audit.

compromised.desc: CRONENBERG SPIDER gains initial access via OAuth device code phishing. morty.smith@synaplexbio.com completes MFA for the attacker at the legitimate Microsoft devicelogin portal, granting a 90-day refresh token to threat actor polling server at 203.0.113.201.

detected.desc: SOC L2 analyst declares IR-SZECHUAN-SAUCE after pivoting from a commercial TI feed alert flagging portal-updates.rickportal-cdn[.]io as CRONENBERG SPIDER C2. Retroactive SIEM match traced compromise to 2026-06-04; 13-day detection gap confirmed.

contained.desc: CrowdStrike Falcon network quarantine applied to host:SCHWIFTY-WKS-01 and host:MEESEEKS-SRV-01. Entra ID sessions revoked for morty.smith@synaplexbio.com and rick.sanchez@synaplexbio.com. NeuraSyncPro service principal deleted. Firewall blocks applied for 192.0.2.0/24, 198.51.100.0/24, 203.0.113.0/24.

eradicated.desc: host:SCHWIFTY-WKS-01 and host:MEESEEKS-SRV-01 reimaged. Scheduled task MicrosoftEdgeUpdate(Szechuan) removed from host:PICKLE-DC-01. NTFS ADS payload removed. krbtgt double-rotated (12-hour interval). Seven unauthorized OAuth apps removed from Entra ID tenant.

recovered.desc: host:SCHWIFTY-WKS-01 returned to production Jun 19. host:MEESEEKS-SRV-01 returned Jun 20. host:PICKLE-DC-01 post-rotation monitoring complete. host:GAZORPAZORP-FS-01 returned after filesystem integrity scan. Business operations confirmed normal Jun 21.

A-initial.desc: morty.smith@synaplexbio.com; device code phished via code SZCH-M0RTY at devicelogin.microsoft.com; 90-day refresh token issued to 203.0.113.201
NeuraSyncPro OAuth app registered from 192.0.2.44; morty.smith@synaplexbio.com consent granted: Mail.ReadWrite, Files.ReadWrite.All, offline_access

A-post.desc:
snadmin PtH: host:SCHWIFTY-WKS-01 → host:MEESEEKS-SRV-01 via SMB Logon Type 3; LSASS dump by pickle_dump.exe
SHA256: 4e7a0d3f6c9b2e5d8a1c4f7e0d3b6c9f2e5a8d1b4f7c0e3a6d9b2f5e8c1a4d7b
MD5: 3d4e5f6a7b8c9d0e1f2a3b4c5d6e7f8a
Kerberoast: SPN HTTP/portal-wubba.synaplexbio.local from host:MEESEEKS-SRV-01; RC4-HMAC etype 0x17; svc-portal-wubba@synaplexbio.com is Domain Admin
DCSync via DRSUAPI: svc-portal-wubba@synaplexbio.com from host:MEESEEKS-SRV-01 → host:PICKLE-DC-01; krbtgt + built-in Admin NT hashes captured
Golden Ticket forged from krbtgt hash on host:MEESEEKS-SRV-01; 10-year TTL; DA + EA + SA groups; full domain compromise

A-impact.desc: Entra ID tokens revoked for morty.smith + rick.sanchez; NeuraSyncPro SP deleted; svc-portal-wubba removed from DA; krbtgt + Admin reset

B-initial.desc:
DARPA-BBB-FY26-Amendment.pdf → meeseeks_loader.dll on host:SCHWIFTY-WKS-01 via regsvr32.exe Squiblydoo; process-hollowed into svchost.exe
SHA256: 3a7f2e8d1b4c9f0e6a3d5b8f1c4e7a0d2b5f8c1e4a7d0b3f6c9e2a5d8b1f4e7a
SHA1: a1b2c3d4e5f6a7b8c9d0e1f2a3b4c5d6e7f8a9b0
MD5: f1e2d3c4b5a69781e2d3c4b5a6978100

B-post.desc: rickdrvr.sys (RTCore64 BYOVD) on host:SCHWIFTY-WKS-01; removed Falcon PsSetLoadImageNotifyRoutine + PsSetCreateProcessNotifyRoutine; opened LSASS dump window
Sched task 'MicrosoftEdgeUpdate(Szechuan)' on host:PICKLE-DC-01; wscript.exe → update-helper.vbs → szechuan_beacon.exe from NTFS ADS on svchost.exe

B-impact.desc: szechuan_beacon.exe as NTFS ADS on C:\Windows\System32\svchost.exe:szechuan; loader: C:\ProgramData\Microsoft\EdgeUpdater\update-helper.vbs

C-initial.desc: Phish from szechuan-auth.portal-secure[.]com to morty.smith@synaplexbio.com; code SZCH-M0RTY; SPF/DKIM pass via compromised relay at 203.0.113.14
szechuan_beacon.exe in svchost.exe on host:SCHWIFTY-WKS-01 → portal-updates.rickportal-cdn[.]io:443; 300s ±15% jitter; Malleable C2 mimics M365 traffic

C-post.desc: C2 blended with M365 sync from host:SCHWIFTY-WKS-01; DGA fallback: blipschitz-analytics[.]net; JA3 mismatched known Microsoft clients, only visible under full TLS inspection

C-impact.desc: 47.3 GB wubba-lubba.7z → 198.51.100.77 direct-IP HTTPS; 32 MB chunks; 50-55 Mbps; ~2.3 hrs; no DNS; firewall egress alert triggered

D-initial.desc: morty.smith@synaplexbio.com token from 192.0.2.44 enumerated 338 accounts, 14 SharePoint libraries; flagged: /NeuraRoute-Research, /DARPA-CUI
NeuraSyncPro paginated morty.smith mailbox from 192.0.2.44; 2,417 emails collected; DARPA threads, lab reports, helpdesk credential resets

D-post.desc: NeuraSyncPro enumerated /sites/NeuraRoute-Research from 192.0.2.44; 4,312 docs ~48 GB: synthesis protocols, Phase III trial data, DARPA CUI

pickle_dump.exe on host:SCHWIFTY-WKS-01: 12 NTLM hashes from LSASS; snadmin DA + 11 cached; Falcon offline via BYOVD
SHA256: 4e7a0d3f6c9b2e5d8a1c4f7e0d3b6c9f2e5a8d1b4f7c0e3a6d9b2f5e8c1a4d7b
MD5: 3d4e5f6a7b8c9d0e1f2a3b4c5d6e7f8a
pluton_extract.exe via Golden Ticket SMB → host:GAZORPAZORP-FS-01; archived \\NeuraResearch\ + \\DARPA-CUI\ into wubba-lubba.7z AES-256

D-impact.desc: wubba-lubba.7z (47.3 GB, AES-256) staged at C:\ProgramData\Microsoft\ on host:GAZORPAZORP-FS-01; held 6 days to avoid egress threshold alerts
47.3 GB wubba-lubba.7z confirmed exfil to 198.51.100.77: NeuraRoute BBB Phase III, synthesis protocols, preclinical data, DARPA CUI deliverables

ir-timeline.title: Spearphishing email delivered to morty.smith@synaplexbio.com
ir-timeline.body: Email 'Action Required: Verify Your Microsoft 365 Account (Code: SZCH-M0RTY)' sent from szechuan-auth.portal-secure[.]com via compromised bulk relay at 203.0.113.14. Passed SPF/DKIM. No malicious attachment or link; device code only; so MDO detonation sandbox returned clean. Victim received the lure on host:SCHWIFTY-WKS-01. Source: Defender for O365 / Exchange Message Trace. T1566.002.
ir-timeline.label: C · Initial Connection/Access
ir-timeline.badge: bg-purple-100 dark:bg-purple-900 dark:bg-opacity-50 text-purple-700 dark:text-purple-300 border border-purple-300 dark:border-purple-700

ir-timeline.title: Device code phishing; morty.smith authenticates for attacker
ir-timeline.body: CRONENBERG SPIDER phishing lure instructed morty.smith@synaplexbio.com to enter code SZCH-M0RTY at the legitimate Microsoft devicelogin portal. Victim completed MFA; fulfilling the attacker's OAuth polling session from 203.0.113.201. Entra ID issued access token + 90-day refresh token to the attacker IP. No Conditional Access policy governed the /devicecode endpoint. Identity Protection risk score: none. Attacker now holds silent persistent cloud access with zero password knowledge. T1566.002, T1528.
ir-timeline.label: A · Initial Connection/Access
ir-timeline.badge: bg-sky-100 dark:bg-sky-900 dark:bg-opacity-50 text-sky-700 dark:text-sky-300 border border-sky-300 dark:border-sky-700

ir-timeline.title: Entra ID: refresh token issued to 203.0.113.201; risk level: none
ir-timeline.body: Entra ID sign-in logs record a successful interactive authentication for morty.smith@synaplexbio.com from 203.0.113.201. Application: 'Microsoft Authentication Broker.' Token issued with 90-day refresh TTL. Identity Protection risk engine assigns 'none'; device code flow lacks the behavioral signals that trigger IP heuristics. No alert generated. Analyst note: this is the earliest pivotable timestamp; all subsequent cloud activity by the attacker uses tokens derived from this session.
ir-timeline.label: ℹ️ Detail
ir-timeline.badge: bg-slate-100 dark:bg-slate-700 text-slate-600 dark:text-slate-300 border border-slate-300 dark:border-slate-600

ir-timeline.title: Graph API tenant enumeration; 338 accounts, 14 SharePoint document libraries
ir-timeline.body: morty.smith@synaplexbio.com access token used from 192.0.2.44 to call: GET /users (338 accounts returned), GET /sites (14 document libraries, flagging /sites/NeuraRoute-Research and /sites/DARPA-BBB-Contract as high-value targets), GET /groups, GET /drives. All calls HTTPS to graph.microsoft.com. Defender for Cloud Apps UEBA flagged 'Impossible Travel' (user physically in San Diego, Graph activity from Eastern Europe); alert auto-suppressed by misconfigured suppression rule. T1087.004, T1526.
ir-timeline.label: D · Initial Connection/Access
ir-timeline.badge: bg-red-100 dark:bg-red-900 dark:bg-opacity-50 text-red-700 dark:text-red-300 border border-red-300 dark:border-red-700

ir-timeline.title: NeuraSyncPro rogue OAuth app registered; morty.smith consent granted
ir-timeline.body: Attacker registered multi-tenant OAuth app 'NeuraSyncPro' in SNI Entra ID tenant via MS Graph POST /applications from 192.0.2.44. App configured with client secret and delegated scopes: Mail.ReadWrite, Files.ReadWrite.All, offline_access. morty.smith@synaplexbio.com clicked attacker-crafted consent URL and granted delegated access; enabling persistent Graph API access independent of credential rotation. Admin consent workflow was not enforced for high-risk scopes. This creates a second, independent persistence mechanism alongside the stolen refresh token. T1671.
ir-timeline.label: A · Initial Connection/Access
ir-timeline.badge: bg-sky-100 dark:bg-sky-900 dark:bg-opacity-50 text-sky-700 dark:text-sky-300 border border-sky-300 dark:border-sky-700

ir-timeline.title: 2,417 emails collected from morty.smith mailbox via NeuraSyncPro app
ir-timeline.body: NeuraSyncPro app used morty.smith@synaplexbio.com refresh token from 192.0.2.44 to execute paginated GET /me/messages calls overnight, downloading 2,417 emails. Collection included DARPA project status update threads, lab report attachments, and an IT helpdesk email containing plaintext VPN credentials for the SNI remote access gateway. Defender for Cloud Apps 'Mass Download' policy triggered; auto-resolved by misconfigured suppression rule targeting NeuraSyncPro. T1114.002, T1119.
ir-timeline.label: D · Initial Connection/Access
ir-timeline.badge: bg-red-100 dark:bg-red-900 dark:bg-opacity-50 text-red-700 dark:text-red-300 border border-red-300 dark:border-red-700

ir-timeline.title: NeuraRoute-Research SharePoint library indexed; 4,312 documents, ~48 GB
ir-timeline.body: NeuraSyncPro app recursively enumerated /sites/NeuraRoute-Research SharePoint library via GET /drives/{id}/root/children from 192.0.2.44. 4,312 documents (~48 GB) indexed including NeuraRoute BBB synthesis protocols, Phase III trial data, and a DARPA CUI-designated deliverable. Document metadata extracted to prioritize highest-value files for staged download. Defender for Cloud Apps 'Suspicious Application Governance' alert auto-suppressed. T1213.002.
ir-timeline.label: D · Post-Breach
ir-timeline.badge: bg-red-100 dark:bg-red-900 dark:bg-opacity-50 text-red-700 dark:text-red-300 border border-red-300 dark:border-red-700

ir-timeline.title: meeseeks_loader.dll; PDF exploit, Squiblydoo, process hollowing on host:SCHWIFTY-WKS-01
ir-timeline.body: DARPA-BBB-FY26-Amendment.pdf exploit dropped meeseeks_loader.dll (SHA256: 3a7f2e8d1b4c9f0e6a3d5b8f1c4e7a0d2b5f8c1e4a7d0b3f6c9e2a5d8b1f4e7a) into AppData Temp on host:SCHWIFTY-WKS-01. Loader executed via regsvr32.exe /s /u /i: (Squiblydoo bypass, evades AppLocker). szechuan_beacon.exe shellcode injected into spawned svchost.exe via process hollowing; no disk artifact for the beacon after initial dropper execution. CrowdStrike Falcon: process tree regsvr32.exe → svchost.exe flagged HIGH. Host targeted: host:SCHWIFTY-WKS-01. T1203, T1218.010, T1055.012.
ir-timeline.label: B · Initial Connection/Access
ir-timeline.badge: bg-orange-100 dark:bg-orange-900 dark:bg-opacity-50 text-orange-700 dark:text-orange-300 border border-orange-300 dark:border-orange-700

ir-timeline.title: Falcon HIGH alert: process hollowing regsvr32→svchost on host:SCHWIFTY-WKS-01; NOT ACTIONED
ir-timeline.body: CrowdStrike Falcon generated severity-HIGH 'Suspicious Process Hollowing' alert on host:SCHWIFTY-WKS-01, flagging the regsvr32.exe → svchost.exe process tree created by meeseeks_loader.dll. Alert placed in SOC queue at 15:03 UTC. Sensor group configured in Detect-only mode (not Prevention). Due to shift-change backlog this alert was not acknowledged for 11 days. This is the critical detection failure of the incident; the entire lateral movement, credential theft, BYOVD, and exfiltration chain unfolded while this alert sat in queue.
ir-timeline.label: ⚠️ Alert; NOT ACTIONED
ir-timeline.badge: bg-amber-100 dark:bg-amber-900 dark:bg-opacity-50 text-amber-700 dark:text-amber-300 border border-amber-300 dark:border-amber-700

ir-timeline.title: C2 beacon established; portal-updates.rickportal-cdn[.]io from host:SCHWIFTY-WKS-01
ir-timeline.body: szechuan_beacon.exe (memory-resident inside svchost.exe on host:SCHWIFTY-WKS-01) made first check-in to portal-updates.rickportal-cdn[.]io resolving to 192.0.2.44, over HTTPS/443. Beacon interval 300 seconds with ±15% jitter. Malleable C2 profile mimics Microsoft Graph API traffic; paths like /v1.0/me/drive/root/children, User-Agent: Microsoft Office/16.0. JA3 TLS fingerprint mismatch from known Microsoft clients visible in full TLS inspection logs. Proxy URL category: 'Content Delivery Network'; not blocked. T1071.001, T1568.002.
ir-timeline.label: C · Initial Connection/Access
ir-timeline.badge: bg-purple-100 dark:bg-purple-900 dark:bg-opacity-50 text-purple-700 dark:text-purple-300 border border-purple-300 dark:border-purple-700

ir-timeline.title: BYOVD: rickdrvr.sys removes Falcon kernel callbacks; EDR blinded on host:SCHWIFTY-WKS-01
ir-timeline.body: rickdrvr.sys (SHA256: 9b2e5d8a1c4f7e0d3b6a9c2f5e8b1d4a7c0f3e6b9d2a5f8c1e4b7a0d3f6c9e2b, modified RTCore64 variant) dropped to C:\Windows\System32\drivers\ on host:SCHWIFTY-WKS-01. Service 'RickDrvr' (kernel mode) created via Service Control Manager; Windows Event 7045. Vulnerable IOCTL codes exploited to remove Falcon's PsSetLoadImageNotifyRoutine and PsSetCreateProcessNotifyRoutine kernel callback registrations. Falcon sensor stopped reporting telemetry 6 minutes later. From this point host:SCHWIFTY-WKS-01 is dark to EDR. T1562.001, T1543.003, T1068.
ir-timeline.label: B · Post-Breach
ir-timeline.badge: bg-orange-100 dark:bg-orange-900 dark:bg-opacity-50 text-orange-700 dark:text-orange-300 border border-orange-300 dark:border-orange-700

ir-timeline.title: CrowdStrike Falcon sensor offline; host:SCHWIFTY-WKS-01
ir-timeline.body: Falcon sensor on host:SCHWIFTY-WKS-01 stopped sending telemetry. Console shows 'Last Seen: 2026-06-08 01:38:51 UTC.' Falcon operational 'Offline Sensor' alert sent to ops@ email alias; no automated network containment or escalation configured for sensor-offline events. Host goes dark to EDR. All subsequent activity on host:SCHWIFTY-WKS-01 through June 17 was reconstructed post-incident from forensic memory analysis and Windows event logs (where available).
ir-timeline.label: ⚠️ Alert
ir-timeline.badge: bg-amber-100 dark:bg-amber-900 dark:bg-opacity-50 text-amber-700 dark:text-amber-300 border border-amber-300 dark:border-amber-700

ir-timeline.title: C2 maintained 11 days; malleable profile evades proxy URL categorization
ir-timeline.body: szechuan_beacon.exe ran memory-resident in svchost.exe on host:SCHWIFTY-WKS-01 from 2026-06-06 through 2026-06-17. Traffic to portal-updates.rickportal-cdn[.]io blended with M365 sync traffic patterns in proxy category logs; proxy categorized the domain as CDN. DGA fallback contacted blipschitz-analytics[.]net when primary was unreachable. JA3 fingerprint mismatch was only visible under full HTTPS inspection (TLS interception not enabled on this proxy segment). T1071.001, T1568.002.
ir-timeline.label: C · Post-Breach
ir-timeline.badge: bg-purple-100 dark:bg-purple-900 dark:bg-opacity-50 text-purple-700 dark:text-purple-300 border border-purple-300 dark:border-purple-700

ir-timeline.title: Pass-the-Hash: snadmin NTLM hash replayed to host:MEESEEKS-SRV-01
ir-timeline.body: pickle_dump.exe (SHA256: 4e7a0d3f6c9b2e5d8a1c4f7e0d3b6c9f2e5a8d1b4f7c0e3a6d9b2f5e8c1a4d7b, Mimikatz fork with obfuscated function names) extracted domain admin snadmin NTLM credential hash from LSASS memory on host:SCHWIFTY-WKS-01 while Falcon was offline. Hash replayed via SMB Logon Type 3 to host:MEESEEKS-SRV-01 without cracking the plaintext. Windows Security Event 4624 generated on host:MEESEEKS-SRV-01. Defender for Identity 'Suspected Pass-the-Hash' alert fired 8 minutes post-event; placed in queue, not actioned. T1003.001, T1550.002.
ir-timeline.label: A · Post-Breach
ir-timeline.badge: bg-sky-100 dark:bg-sky-900 dark:bg-opacity-50 text-sky-700 dark:text-sky-300 border border-sky-300 dark:border-sky-700

ir-timeline.title: LSASS dumped by pickle_dump.exe on host:SCHWIFTY-WKS-01
ir-timeline.body: pickle_dump.exe (SHA256: 4e7a0d3f6c9b2e5d8a1c4f7e0d3b6c9f2e5a8d1b4f7c0e3a6d9b2f5e8c1a4d7b) extracted 12 cached NTLM hashes from LSASS on host:SCHWIFTY-WKS-01, including domain admin snadmin. Falcon was offline due to rickdrvr.sys BYOVD; no EDR visibility during the dump. Only discovered post-incident via forensic memory analysis of host:SCHWIFTY-WKS-01 disk image. T1003.001.
ir-timeline.label: D · Post-Breach
ir-timeline.badge: bg-red-100 dark:bg-red-900 dark:bg-opacity-50 text-red-700 dark:text-red-300 border border-red-300 dark:border-red-700

ir-timeline.title: Kerberoasting: svc-portal-wubba TGS requested, RC4-HMAC downgrade
ir-timeline.body: From host:MEESEEKS-SRV-01, attacker requested TGS for SPN HTTP/portal-wubba.synaplexbio.local. RC4-HMAC etype 0x17 returned due to missing AES enforcement policy on the account. svc-portal-wubba@synaplexbio.com is a Domain Admin member; a longstanding misconfiguration. TGS ticket exfiltrated for offline GPU cracking; password cracked in approximately 40 minutes. Windows Security Event 4769 on host:PICKLE-DC-01. Defender for Identity 'Kerberoasting' alert fired; not actioned. T1558.003.
ir-timeline.label: A · Post-Breach
ir-timeline.badge: bg-sky-100 dark:bg-sky-900 dark:bg-opacity-50 text-sky-700 dark:text-sky-300 border border-sky-300 dark:border-sky-700

ir-timeline.title: DCSync: krbtgt and Administrator hashes replicated from host:PICKLE-DC-01
ir-timeline.body: svc-portal-wubba@synaplexbio.com (cracked DA account) used DRSUAPI replication rights to replicate host:PICKLE-DC-01 from host:MEESEEKS-SRV-01. krbtgt NT hash and built-in Administrator NT hash captured. Windows Security Event 4662 (Directory Service Replication) generated on host:PICKLE-DC-01 but alerting was not configured on this DC. Defender for Identity 'Suspected DCSync Attack' alert fired; not actioned. T1003.006.
ir-timeline.label: A · Post-Breach
ir-timeline.badge: bg-sky-100 dark:bg-sky-900 dark:bg-opacity-50 text-sky-700 dark:text-sky-300 border border-sky-300 dark:border-sky-700

ir-timeline.title: Golden Ticket forged; 10-year TTL, full domain compromise achieved
ir-timeline.body: krbtgt NT hash used to forge Kerberos Golden Ticket with 10-year TTL, SNI domain SID, and group membership in Domain Admins + Enterprise Admins + Schema Admins on host:MEESEEKS-SRV-01. Ticket injected into active LSASS session via pass-the-ticket. Defender for Identity 'Golden Ticket Usage' alert fired on anomalous TGT TTL; not actioned. From this point the attacker had unconstrained persistence across ALL SNI Active Directory resources including host:PICKLE-DC-01 and host:GAZORPAZORP-FS-01. T1558.001.
ir-timeline.label: A · Post-Breach
ir-timeline.badge: bg-sky-100 dark:bg-sky-900 dark:bg-opacity-50 text-sky-700 dark:text-sky-300 border border-sky-300 dark:border-sky-700

ir-timeline.title: Attacker gained unrestricted, undetected access across our network (Exec Version)
ir-timeline.body:
The attacker forged a master credential that let them impersonate any employee or system company-wide, completely undetected, for up to ten years. Our security tools did flag this as suspicious, but the alert wasn't acted on in time. From this point on, the attacker could move freely through nearly every part of our IT environment.

Demo tip: editing this note directly in the Presentation Timeline does not write back to the Working Timeline table below — the two only sync one-way, at creation. You can also build alternate “exec briefing” versions of any event like this one, and swap which version is shown on the Presentation Timeline without touching the original.
ir-timeline.label: A · Post-Breach
ir-timeline.badge: bg-sky-100 dark:bg-sky-900 dark:bg-opacity-50 text-sky-700 dark:text-sky-300 border border-sky-300 dark:border-sky-700

ir-timeline.title: Persistence: scheduled task 'MicrosoftEdgeUpdate(Szechuan)' on host:PICKLE-DC-01
ir-timeline.body: Scheduled task created on host:PICKLE-DC-01 via schtasks.exe using the Golden Ticket session. Name: MicrosoftEdgeUpdate(Szechuan). Triggers: system startup + every 4 hours. Action: wscript.exe executes C:\ProgramData\Microsoft\EdgeUpdater\update-helper.vbs which loads and runs szechuan_beacon.exe from NTFS Alternate Data Stream. Windows Security Event 4698 generated. Sysmon Event 11 (file creation in ProgramData). Task name designed to blend with legitimate Microsoft Edge update scheduling. T1053.005, T1564.004.
ir-timeline.label: B · Post-Breach
ir-timeline.badge: bg-orange-100 dark:bg-orange-900 dark:bg-opacity-50 text-orange-700 dark:text-orange-300 border border-orange-300 dark:border-orange-700

ir-timeline.title: pluton_extract.exe archives NeuraRoute + DARPA-CUI shares on host:GAZORPAZORP-FS-01
ir-timeline.body: pluton_extract.exe (SHA256: 7d0a3f6c9b2e5d8a1c4f7e0b3a6d9c2f5b8e1d4a7c0f3e6b9d2a5f8c1e4b7a0d, custom 7-Zip AES-256 wrapper with embedded key) deployed to host:GAZORPAZORP-FS-01 via SMB using the Golden Ticket session. Recursively archived \\GAZORPAZORP-FS-01\NeuraResearch\ and \\GAZORPAZORP-FS-01\DARPA-CUI\ targeting files modified in the past 24 months (.docx, .xlsx, .pdf, .ipynb, .pkl, .h5, .zip). DLP sensor generated 'bulk file read by unusual process' alert; routed to unmanned distribution list. T1560.001, T1039.
ir-timeline.label: D · Post-Breach
ir-timeline.badge: bg-red-100 dark:bg-red-900 dark:bg-opacity-50 text-red-700 dark:text-red-300 border border-red-300 dark:border-red-700

ir-timeline.title: ADS beacon on host:PICKLE-DC-01; persists beyond credential rotation
ir-timeline.body: szechuan_beacon.exe payload stored as NTFS Alternate Data Stream on C:\Windows\System32\svchost.exe:szechuan on host:PICKLE-DC-01. update-helper.vbs at C:\ProgramData\Microsoft\EdgeUpdater\ executes via the scheduled task MicrosoftEdgeUpdate(Szechuan). This backdoor survives standard account password resets; only krbtgt double-rotation (12-hour interval) combined with scheduled task removal AND ADS cleanup achieves full eradication. Eradication team must confirm all three components independently. T1564.004.
ir-timeline.label: B · Impact
ir-timeline.badge: bg-orange-100 dark:bg-orange-900 dark:bg-opacity-50 text-orange-700 dark:text-orange-300 border border-orange-300 dark:border-orange-700

ir-timeline.title: wubba-lubba.7z staged on host:GAZORPAZORP-FS-01; 47.3 GB AES-256 archive
ir-timeline.body: pluton_extract.exe completed archival and wrote wubba-lubba.7z (47.3 GB, AES-256 password) to C:\ProgramData\Microsoft\ on host:GAZORPAZORP-FS-01. Archive password makes content inspection without the key infeasible. Staged for 6 days before transmission; deliberate patience to avoid triggering egress volume anomaly thresholds. DLP alert was generated but not seen until Jun 17 post-declaration sweep. T1560.001.
ir-timeline.label: D · Impact
ir-timeline.badge: bg-red-100 dark:bg-red-900 dark:bg-opacity-50 text-red-700 dark:text-red-300 border border-red-300 dark:border-red-700

ir-timeline.title: 47.3 GB exfiltrated to 198.51.100.77 via direct-IP HTTPS
ir-timeline.body: wubba-lubba.7z (47.3 GB, AES-256) transmitted in 32 MB chunks over ~2.3 hours via direct-IP HTTPS POST to 198.51.100.77 from host:GAZORPAZORP-FS-01. No DNS resolution; bypasses proxy URL category lookup entirely. NetFlow: sustained 50–55 Mbps outbound from host:GAZORPAZORP-FS-01 to 198.51.100.77 over port 443. Firewall egress anomaly alert fired on unusual direct-IP HTTPS volume; this alert triggered the IR declaration. Contents confirmed: NeuraRoute BBB Phase III dataset, synthesis protocols, DARPA CUI deliverables. SNI legal notified. DFARS 252.204-7012 breach reporting initiated (72-hour window). T1048.002.
ir-timeline.label: C · Impact
ir-timeline.badge: bg-purple-100 dark:bg-purple-900 dark:bg-opacity-50 text-purple-700 dark:text-purple-300 border border-purple-300 dark:border-purple-700

ir-timeline.title: 47 gigabytes of sensitive research data stolen (Exec Version)
ir-timeline.body: Over roughly two and a half hours, the attacker copied and removed the equivalent of tens of thousands of pages of sensitive research data, including materials tied to a government research contract. Our firewall detected the unusual data transfer, and that alert is what triggered our formal incident response. Legal has already been notified given the regulatory reporting requirements involved.
ir-timeline.label: C · Impact
ir-timeline.badge: bg-purple-100 dark:bg-purple-900 dark:bg-opacity-50 text-purple-700 dark:text-purple-300 border border-purple-300 dark:border-purple-700

ir-timeline.title: NeuraRoute Phase III dataset + DARPA CUI confirmed exfiltrated
ir-timeline.body: Forensic and NetFlow analysis confirmed full 47.3 GB wubba-lubba.7z transmitted to 198.51.100.77. Contents confirmed via host:GAZORPAZORP-FS-01 file server audit log: NeuraRoute BBB nano-delivery Phase III research, synthesis protocols, preclinical trial data, and CUI-marked DARPA deliverables. SNI legal team notified. DARPA contracting officer notified Jun 18. Mandatory CUI breach reporting timeline initiated per DFARS 252.204-7012 (72-hour window). This represents the full impact event of the intrusion.

ir-timeline.badge: bg-red-100 dark:bg-red-900 dark:bg-opacity-50 text-red-700 dark:text-red-300 border border-red-300 dark:border-red-700

ir-timeline.title: TI feed: portal-updates.rickportal-cdn[.]io flagged as CRONENBERG SPIDER C2
ir-timeline.body: Commercial threat intelligence platform released IOC feed at 10:45 UTC flagging portal-updates.rickportal-cdn[.]io as a confirmed CRONENBERG SPIDER C2 infrastructure domain. SIEM ingested feed at 11:03 UTC and retroactively matched proxy log entries from host:SCHWIFTY-WKS-01 going back to 2026-06-06. SIEM P2 correlation alert fired and created SOC ticket; the proximate trigger for the IR declaration. The 13-day detection gap is attributable entirely to the unactioned Falcon HIGH alert from Jun 6.
ir-timeline.label: 🔍 Intel

ir-timeline.title: SOC L2 analyst declares SZECHUAN SAUCE, Severity 1
ir-timeline.body: SOC L2 analyst pulled SIEM alert, traced proxy hits to host:SCHWIFTY-WKS-01 and rick.sanchez@synaplexbio.com. Pivoted to Entra ID sign-in logs in Sentinel, identified Jun 4 device code auth from 203.0.113.201 for morty.smith@synaplexbio.com; unfamiliar country, app 'Microsoft Authentication Broker.' Escalated to IR team. Severity 1 declared at 12:15 UTC. Incident SZECHUAN SAUCE opened.
ir-timeline.label: 🔍 SOC Detection
ir-timeline.badge: bg-green-100 dark:bg-green-900 dark:bg-opacity-50 text-green-700 dark:text-green-300 border border-green-300 dark:border-green-700

ir-timeline.title: Containment: Entra ID sessions revoked, NeuraSyncPro deleted, CA policy deployed
ir-timeline.body: CrowdStrike Falcon network quarantine applied to host:SCHWIFTY-WKS-01 and host:MEESEEKS-SRV-01. All active Entra ID tokens revoked for morty.smith@synaplexbio.com and rick.sanchez@synaplexbio.com. NeuraSyncPro service principal deleted; all delegated consent grants revoked. svc-portal-wubba@synaplexbio.com removed from Domain Admins, password reset. Emergency Conditional Access policy blocking device code flow for all non-privileged users deployed. Admin consent requirement enforced for high-risk OAuth scopes (Mail.ReadWrite, Files.ReadWrite.All). Firewall blocks: 192.0.2.0/24, 198.51.100.0/24, 203.0.113.0/24.

M.text: szechuan_beacon.exe; memory-resident C2 implant inside svchost.exe on host:SCHWIFTY-WKS-01 via process hollowing; no disk artifact after meeseeks_loader.dll execution
pickle_dump.exe; Mimikatz fork loaded into LSASS process context on host:SCHWIFTY-WKS-01 while Falcon was blinded; extracted 12 NTLM hashes including snadmin DA
rickdrvr.sys; kernel-mode driver on host:SCHWIFTY-WKS-01; removed Falcon PsSetLoadImageNotifyRoutine and PsSetCreateProcessNotifyRoutine callback arrays from kernel via RTCore64 IOCTLs

I.text: morty.smith@synaplexbio.com; primary victim; device code phished 2026-06-04, 90-day OAuth token stolen, mailbox (2,417 emails) and SharePoint research files exfiltrated via NeuraSyncPro app
rick.sanchez@synaplexbio.com; CTO; received DARPA-BBB-FY26-Amendment.pdf 2026-06-06; primary endpoint foothold on host:SCHWIFTY-WKS-01
svc-portal-wubba@synaplexbio.com; Kerberoastable DA service account (SPN: HTTP/portal-wubba.synaplexbio.local); cracked in ~40 min via RC4-HMAC downgrade; used for DCSync against host:PICKLE-DC-01
host:SCHWIFTY-WKS-01; primary endpoint; initial foothold, BYOVD, LSASS dump, memory-resident C2 beacon throughout dwell period
host:MEESEEKS-SRV-01; lateral movement target via PtH; Golden Ticket injected here; used as pivot for DCSync and further AD operations
host:PICKLE-DC-01; domain controller; target of DCSync; ADS beacon + scheduled task persistence installed; krbtgt hash captured here
host:GAZORPAZORP-FS-01; file server; NeuraRoute and DARPA-CUI shares archived by pluton_extract.exe; wubba-lubba.7z staged here 6 days before exfiltration

N.text: portal-updates.rickportal-cdn[.]io; primary C2; confirmed CRONENBERG SPIDER IOC per TI feed 2026-06-17; resolves to 192.0.2.44; beacon interval 300s ±15% jitter from host:SCHWIFTY-WKS-01
198.51.100.77; exfiltration receiver; 47.3 GB wubba-lubba.7z transmitted via direct-IP HTTPS, no DNS; sustained 50–55 Mbps egress from host:GAZORPAZORP-FS-01
203.0.113.201; device code phishing polling server; generated OAuth device authorization code and polled Microsoft /token endpoint after victim morty.smith@synaplexbio.com completed MFA
blipschitz-analytics[.]net; DGA fallback C2; contacted by szechuan_beacon.exe on host:SCHWIFTY-WKS-01 when portal-updates.rickportal-cdn[.]io unreachable
szechuan-auth.portal-secure[.]com; phishing sender domain; delivered device code lure to morty.smith@synaplexbio.com; passed SPF/DKIM via compromised bulk relay at 203.0.113.14

D.text: meeseeks_loader.dll; SHA256: 3a7f2e8d1b4c9f0e6a3d5b8f1c4e7a0d2b5f8c1e4a7d0b3f6c9e2a5d8b1f4e7a; initial dropper on host:SCHWIFTY-WKS-01; Squiblydoo via regsvr32.exe; hollows szechuan_beacon.exe into svchost.exe

rickdrvr.sys; SHA256: 9b2e5d8a1c4f7e0d3b6a9c2f5e8b1d4a7c0f3e6b9d2a5f8c1e4b7a0d3f6c9e2b; BYOVD kernel driver (modified RTCore64) on host:SCHWIFTY-WKS-01; service name 'RickDrvr'; drops to C:\Windows\System32\drivers\

pluton_extract.exe; SHA256: 7d0a3f6c9b2e5d8a1c4f7e0b3a6d9c2f5b8e1d4a7c0f3e6b9d2a5f8c1e4b7a0d; custom 7-Zip wrapper with embedded AES-256 key; archived NeuraRoute + DARPA-CUI shares on host:GAZORPAZORP-FS-01
wubba-lubba.7z; 47.3 GB AES-256 archive; staged at C:\ProgramData\Microsoft\ on host:GAZORPAZORP-FS-01 for 6 days; transmitted to 198.51.100.77 on 2026-06-17
update-helper.vbs + NTFS ADS szechuan_beacon.exe; persistence loader on host:PICKLE-DC-01; scheduled task MicrosoftEdgeUpdate(Szechuan) invokes VBScript which executes ADS payload via wscript.exe

ir-hunts.title: Additional OAuth service principals survive credential rotation and provide persistent tenant access
ir-hunts.premise: CRONENBERG SPIDER registered NeuraSyncPro during initial access on June 4. Prior campaigns by this actor include registering multiple OAuth apps as redundant persistence mechanisms. If additional apps were registered during the June 4–17 window, they survived the June 17 NeuraSyncPro deletion and retain active 90-day refresh tokens.
ir-hunts.huntScope: Microsoft Entra ID tenant (synaplexbio.com); all app registrations and service principals created after June 1, 2026
ir-hunts.dataSourcesNeeded:
Entra ID Audit Logs (Application Management category)
MS Graph GET /applications and /servicePrincipals (full tenant dump)
M365 Unified Audit Log (consent grant events)
Entra ID Sign-In logs (app-only authentication flows)
ir-hunts.dataSourcesAvailable:
Microsoft Sentinel (EntraID log connector)
M365 Compliance Center

techniques.name: Cloud Application Integration
techniques.expectedIocs:
Service principals created June 4–17 by non-admin accounts
App registrations with scopes: Mail.ReadWrite, Files.ReadWrite.All, Directory.ReadWrite.All
App-only sign-ins to graph.microsoft.com from 192.0.2.x or 203.0.113.x
techniques.notes: Tenant lacked admin consent requirement for high-risk delegated permissions. CRONENBERG SPIDER likely registered additional apps before or after NeuraSyncPro. Any active refresh token from the attack window remains valid until explicitly revoked.
techniques.queryIdeas:
// Sentinel KQL; app registrations during attack window
AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated between (datetime(2026-06-04) .. datetime(2026-06-17))
| where Category == "ApplicationManagement"
| where OperationName in ("Add application", "Add service principal")
| extend InitiatedBy = tostring(InitiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName)
| project TimeGenerated, OperationName, InitiatedBy, TargetResources

techniques.name: Application Access Token
techniques.expectedIocs:
Active OAuth refresh tokens with expiry > 30d issued during June 4–17
App-only sign-ins to Graph API from non-SNI IP ranges post-containment
techniques.notes: OAuth refresh tokens survive standard credential rotation unless explicitly revoked. Token revocation for NeuraSyncPro must be verified via Graph API /oauth2PermissionGrants; deletion of the service principal is necessary but not always sufficient.
techniques.queryIdeas:
// App-only sign-ins from attacker IP ranges
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where AppDisplayName !in (known_approved_apps)
| where IPAddress matches regex @"^(192\.0\.2\.|203\.0\.113\.)"
| project TimeGenerated, UserPrincipalName, AppDisplayName, IPAddress, ResourceDisplayName

ir-hunts.title: morty.smith mailbox access used for internal spearphishing, creating unidentified account compromises
ir-hunts.premise: NeuraSyncPro held Mail.ReadWrite delegated permission to morty.smith@synaplexbio.com from June 4 onward. Internal phishing from a trusted colleague's address bypasses most email gateway controls. If additional SNI employees received and acted on a device code lure from morty.smith, those accounts represent unidentified compromises outside the current IR scope.
ir-hunts.huntScope: All SNI email accounts that received email from morty.smith@synaplexbio.com between June 4–17, 2026
ir-hunts.dataSourcesNeeded:
Exchange Online Message Trace (sent-items from morty.smith June 4–17)
Entra ID Sign-In logs filtered by attacker IP ranges for recipient accounts
Defender for Office 365 Safe Links click telemetry
Defender for Cloud Apps UEBA (peer group anomaly)
ir-hunts.dataSourcesAvailable:
Microsoft Sentinel
M365 Compliance Center
MDO Advanced Hunting

techniques.name: Internal Spearphishing
techniques.expectedIocs:
Outbound emails from morty.smith@synaplexbio.com containing links to szechuan-auth.portal-secure[.]com or device login instructions
Entra ID sign-ins from 203.0.113.201 or 192.0.2.44 for SNI users other than morty.smith and rick.sanchez
techniques.notes: Internal phishing from a legitimate account passes SPF/DKIM and bypasses most email gateway controls. Focus on Entra ID sign-in anomalies in accounts that received email from morty.smith after June 4.
techniques.queryIdeas:
// Emails sent from morty.smith during attack window
OfficeActivity
| where TimeGenerated between (datetime(2026-06-04) .. datetime(2026-06-17))
| where UserId == "morty.smith@synaplexbio.com"
| where Operation in ("Send", "SendAs", "SendOnBehalf")
| project TimeGenerated, Operation, Recipients, ClientIP

techniques.name: Steal Application Access Token
techniques.expectedIocs:
NeuraSyncPro or other CRONENBERG SPIDER app consent events for users other than morty.smith@synaplexbio.com
OAuth app consent grants to App ID: 7c3f9d12-e4b8-4a21-b503-c4d5e6f7a8b9
techniques.notes: If additional device codes were harvested, compromised accounts would also show OAuth consent events for the same attacker-controlled app. Cross-reference consent audit logs with the morty.smith sent-items query.
techniques.queryIdeas:
// Consent grants from non-morty.smith users
AuditLogs
| where OperationName == "Consent to application"
| where TimeGenerated between (datetime(2026-06-04) .. datetime(2026-06-17))
| where tostring(TargetResources) contains "NeuraSyncPro"
| extend InitiatedBy = tostring(InitiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName)
| where InitiatedBy != "morty.smith@synaplexbio.com"
| project TimeGenerated, InitiatedBy, TargetResources

ir-hunts.title: rickdrvr.sys BYOVD deployed on additional domain-joined hosts to blind EDR before lateral movement
ir-hunts.premise: Falcon sensor went offline on host:SCHWIFTY-WKS-01 precisely as lateral movement began on June 8. The actor had Golden Ticket access from June 10 to host:MEESEEKS-SRV-01, host:PICKLE-DC-01, and host:GAZORPAZORP-FS-01. Telemetry gaps on any of these hosts during June 8–17 would be consistent with additional BYOVD deployments.
ir-hunts.huntScope: All domain-joined SNI hosts; prioritize host:MEESEEKS-SRV-01, host:PICKLE-DC-01, host:GAZORPAZORP-FS-01
ir-hunts.dataSourcesNeeded:
Windows System Event ID 7045 (all domain-joined hosts)
CrowdStrike Falcon sensor last-seen inventory; identify telemetry gaps >5 min outside maintenance windows
MDE device inventory; SHA256 hash hunt for rickdrvr.sys
Sysmon Event ID 6 (driver loaded)
ir-hunts.dataSourcesAvailable:
Microsoft Sentinel (Windows Security Events)
CrowdStrike Falcon console
MDE Advanced Hunting

techniques.name: Disable or Modify Tools
techniques.expectedIocs:
rickdrvr.sys SHA256: 9b2e5d8a1c4f7e0d3b6a9c2f5e8b1d4a7c0f3e6b9d2a5f8c1e4b7a0d3f6c9e2b on any host
Falcon heartbeat gaps >5 min on host:MEESEEKS-SRV-01, host:PICKLE-DC-01, host:GAZORPAZORP-FS-01 during June 8–17
Registry key: HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\RickDrvr on any host
techniques.notes: Any host with a Falcon sensor gap during June 8–17 that is NOT in a documented maintenance window should be treated as potentially compromised. Check MDE for secondary coverage if Falcon was blinded.
techniques.queryIdeas:
// MDE hash hunt for rickdrvr.sys
DeviceFileEvents
| where SHA256 == "9b2e5d8a1c4f7e0d3b6a9c2f5e8b1d4a7c0f3e6b9d2a5f8c1e4b7a0d3f6c9e2b"
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, FolderPath, FileName, InitiatingProcessFileName

// Sentinel: kernel driver installs in attack window
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated between (datetime(2026-06-08) .. datetime(2026-06-17))
| where EventID == 7045
| where ServiceType == "kernel mode driver"
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, ServiceName, ServiceFileName

techniques.name: Windows Service
techniques.expectedIocs:
Service name 'RickDrvr' in Windows Event 7045 on any host other than host:SCHWIFTY-WKS-01
Driver file in C:\Windows\System32\drivers\ written by non-SYSTEM principal
techniques.notes: Windows Event 7045 is high-fidelity for this hunt. Filter to kernel mode drivers installed in the June 8–17 window and exclude known-good vendor drivers. Prioritize any driver with a revoked or untrusted Authenticode certificate.
techniques.queryIdeas:
// Event 7045 targeted hunt
SecurityEvent
| where EventID == 7045
| where TimeGenerated between (datetime(2026-06-08) .. datetime(2026-06-17))
| where ServiceType == "kernel mode driver"
| where ServiceName contains "Rick" or ServiceFileName contains "rickdrvr"
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, ServiceName, ServiceFileName

techniques.name: Exploitation for Privilege Escalation
techniques.expectedIocs: rickdrvr.sys or similar LOLDriver variants on host:MEESEEKS-SRV-01, host:PICKLE-DC-01, or host:GAZORPAZORP-FS-01
techniques.notes: The RTCore64 vulnerability used here is documented in the LOLDrivers project. Confirm the driver blocklist in MDE includes all RTCore64 hash variants. WDAC policy enforcement should be verified across all domain-joined hosts.

ir-hunts.title: Golden Ticket Kerberos persistence may allow threat actor re-entry even after password resets, if krbtgt has not been rotated twice with a 10-hour inter-rotation gap
ir-hunts.premise: On or around June 9th, 2026, the threat actor achieved domain administrator-level access and performed DCSync against the Synaplex Active Directory environment. DCSync abuses the MS-DRSR (Directory Replication Service Remote Protocol) to impersonate a domain controller and pull the NTLM hash of any account; including krbtgt; without touching NTDS.dit on disk. The krbtgt hash is the master signing key for all Kerberos authentication in the domain. Armed with it, the attacker can craft a fully valid, arbitrary Kerberos Ticket Granting Ticket (TGT) for any account name, any group membership, and any ticket lifetime, without making any additional requests to a domain controller. User password resets, account lockouts, and disabling compromised accounts have zero effect on the validity of a pre-forged Golden Ticket. The only complete remediation is rotating the krbtgt password twice; with an inter-rotation gap exceeding 10 hours; to fully evict the compromised hash from both the current and previous password slots. Until both rotations are confirmed complete, the attacker retains persistent Kerberos-authenticated access to any service in the domain.
ir-hunts.huntScope: All Active Directory Kerberos authentication traffic on domain controllers, Microsoft Defender for Identity (MDI) alerts, Entra ID sign-in logs (hybrid/cloud Kerberos scenarios), and Windows Security Event Logs on all domain controllers. Covers all logon activity from IP addresses or hosts inconsistent with legitimate user patterns, and specifically any authentication using accounts that have been disabled, renamed, or whose passwords were reset post-incident. All DC, admin jump host, and privileged workstation lateral movement is in scope.
ir-hunts.dataSourcesNeeded:
DC Security Event Logs (Events 4662, 4768, 4769, 4624, 4648); requires Advanced Audit Policy: 'Audit Directory Service Access' SACL on domain object, 'Audit Kerberos Authentication Service', 'Audit Kerberos Service Ticket Operations'
Microsoft Defender for Identity (MDI) sensors on ALL domain controllers; primary high-confidence detection source; performs network-layer Kerberos inspection on port 88
Entra ID Sign-in Logs for hybrid/cloud Kerberos follow-on activity
EDR telemetry (CrowdStrike Falcon); confirm sensor integrity restored after BYOVD incident
Network traffic / Kerberos port 88 captures for PAC and ticket lifetime analysis
ir-hunts.dataSourcesAvailable:
DC Security Event Logs available if forwarded to SIEM; Advanced Audit Policy configuration unconfirmed; DS Access auditing likely not enabled by default
MDI assumed deployed; sensor placement on all DCs must be verified; alert pipeline to Sentinel must be confirmed active and not suppressed
Entra ID logs: available, forwarded to Sentinel by default
EDR: Falcon was disabled by BYOVD driver (rickdrvr.sys); must confirm full re-enable and sensor integrity verification post-remediation
ir-hunts.conclusionStatement: [ANALYST TO COMPLETE]; Based on review of MDI alerts, DC Security Event Logs (Events 4662, 4768, 4769, 4624) for the period June 9 through [date], and confirmation of krbtgt rotation status: [Golden Ticket persistence was / was not] confirmed. krbtgt rotation count as of [date]: [N] rotations performed, inter-rotation gap [met / did not meet] the 10-hour minimum. Hunt status: [open / confirmed / denied / inconclusive].

techniques.name: DCSync
techniques.expectedIocs:
Event ID 4662 on DC where a non-machine account (SubjectUserName not ending in '$') initiates MS-DRSR replication with DS-Replication-Get-Changes-All GUID (1131f6ad-9c07-11d1-f79f-00c04fc2dcd2)
MDI alert: 'Suspected DCSync attack (replication of directory services)'
Process on attacker host: Impacket secretsdump.py, Mimikatz lsadump::dcsync, or similar
Outbound network connection from non-DC host to DC on port 135/445 with RPC MS-DRSR traffic
techniques.notes: Event 4662 requires 'Audit Directory Service Access' SACL explicitly set on the domain root object; NOT enabled in default AD deployments. Confirm this audit category is active on all SNI domain controllers. Azure AD Connect (MSOL_ accounts) and legitimate hybrid sync accounts will fire this event and must be allowlisted after validation. MDI provides out-of-box behavioral detection without custom audit configuration and is the primary detection path.
techniques.queryIdeas:
// DCSync Detection; Event 4662 on DCs (requires DS Access auditing)
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated between (datetime(2026-06-09) .. now())
| where EventID == 4662
| where Properties has "1131f6ad-9c07-11d1-f79f-00c04fc2dcd2"
| where SubjectUserName !endswith "$"
| where SubjectUserName !in ("ANONYMOUS LOGON", "LOCAL SERVICE", "NETWORK SERVICE")
| project TimeGenerated, SubjectUserName, SubjectDomainName, Computer, ObjectName, Properties
| sort by TimeGenerated desc

// MDI alert pivot
SecurityAlert
| where TimeGenerated between (datetime(2026-06-09) .. now())
| where ProductName == "Azure Advanced Threat Protection"
| where AlertName has_any ("DCSync", "Replication", "directory services")
| project TimeGenerated, AlertName, AlertSeverity, Entities, Description

techniques.name: Golden Ticket
techniques.expectedIocs:
Kerberos TGS requests (Event 4769) with no corresponding TGT request (Event 4768) from the same source IP in a 2-hour window
RC4-HMAC encryption type (0x17) on TGS requests in a domain enforcing AES256 (0x12); anomalous if Mimikatz/Impacket default was used
Abnormally long ticket lifetime in Event 4769 (Mimikatz default: 10 years; legitimate max: 10 hours)
Event 4768 with failure code 0x6 (KDC_ERR_C_PRINCIPAL_UNKNOWN) followed by successful 4769 for same apparent account; nonexistent account in forged ticket PAC
MDI alerts: 'Suspected Golden Ticket usage (ticket anomaly)' or 'Suspected Golden Ticket usage (nonexistent account)'
techniques.notes: Ticket lifetime and PAC contents are NOT in raw Windows Security Event logs; definitive Golden Ticket identification via events alone is unreliable. MDI's network-layer Kerberos inspection (spanning DC traffic on port 88) is the primary detection mechanism. Confirm MDI sensors are on ALL DC network segments. If domain GPO does not enforce AES-only (many environments still allow RC4 for legacy compatibility), the encryption type anomaly indicator cannot be used. Verify SNI's Kerberos encryption policy before relying on this IOC.
techniques.queryIdeas:
// Golden Ticket: RC4 encryption anomaly (validate domain encryption policy first)
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated between (datetime(2026-06-09) .. now())
| where EventID == 4769
| where EventData has "0x17"  // RC4-HMAC; suspicious if domain enforces AES
| project TimeGenerated, TargetUserName, ServiceName, IpAddress, Computer, EventData
| sort by TimeGenerated desc

// Golden Ticket: TGS with no preceding TGT in same 2hr window
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated between (datetime(2026-06-09) .. now())
| where EventID in (4768, 4769)
| summarize TGT_count = countif(EventID == 4768), TGS_count = countif(EventID == 4769)
    by IpAddress, TargetUserName, bin(TimeGenerated, 2h)
| where TGT_count == 0 and TGS_count > 0
| sort by TimeGenerated desc

// MDI Golden Ticket alerts
SecurityAlert
| where TimeGenerated between (datetime(2026-06-09) .. now())
| where ProductName == "Azure Advanced Threat Protection"
| where AlertName has_any ("Golden Ticket", "ticket anomaly", "nonexistent account", "Forged")
| project TimeGenerated, AlertName, AlertSeverity, Description, Entities

techniques.name: Pass the Ticket
techniques.expectedIocs:
Event 4624 (network logon, LogonType 3) with Kerberos auth from IP inconsistent with account's normal logon baseline
Event 4624 on multiple servers from same source IP in rapid succession; consistent with automated lateral movement
Event 4648 (explicit credential logon) on source host prior to lateral movement chain
Kerberos ticket injected via Mimikatz kerberos::ptt or Rubeus ptt; no dedicated Windows event; surfaces in EDR via LSASS access or process injection
MDI alert: 'Suspected Pass-the-Ticket attack'
Same source IP accessing 5+ distinct target hosts within a 30-minute window using Kerberos auth
techniques.notes: In a Golden Ticket scenario, Pass the Ticket is the delivery mechanism; the attacker injects the forged TGT into their session and uses standard Kerberos to request service tickets from legitimate DCs. Distinguishing legitimate Kerberos lateral movement (IT admins, GPO processing, domain join) from attacker PTT via raw event logs alone is high-noise work. MDI entity profiling and anomaly detection is required for high-confidence results. If the attacker used the forged ticket to access cloud resources via hybrid Kerberos delegation, also investigate Golden SAML (T1606.002) as a related follow-on technique.
techniques.queryIdeas:
// PTT: Kerberos network logons from anomalous sources
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated between (datetime(2026-06-09) .. now())
| where EventID == 4624
| where LogonType == 3
| where AuthenticationPackageName == "Kerberos"
| summarize SourceIPs = make_set(IpAddress), LogonCount = count(), TargetHosts = make_set(Computer)
    by TargetUserName, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where array_length(TargetHosts) > 3
| sort by LogonCount desc

// PTT: Rapid lateral movement; same IP, 5+ hosts in 30 min
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated between (datetime(2026-06-09) .. now())
| where EventID == 4624
| where LogonType == 3
| where AuthenticationPackageName == "Kerberos"
| summarize UniqueTargets = dcount(Computer), LogonEvents = count()
    by IpAddress, TargetUserName, bin(TimeGenerated, 30m)
| where UniqueTargets >= 5 and LogonEvents >= 10
| sort by UniqueTargets desc
